focus: Inventory external make-interfaces-feel-better techniques and map initial coverage against sk-design hub, modes, and shared register.

label: External corpus is tactical micro-craft, not broad visual-direction philosophy
Concentric border radius formula appears net-new as a concrete sk-design rule
Neutral image outline rule appears net-new and highly actionable
target: sk-design foundations or interface preflight plus audit

label: Shadow-as-border recipe is useful but must be reconciled with ghost-card anti-pattern guidance
conflictRisk: Could normalize border-plus-shadow slop unless scoped to depth rings and not decorative card stacking.

label: Contextual icon swap exact opacity/scale/blur fallback is under-covered
Root font smoothing is net-new in targeted sk-design coverage
Text-wrap exists in foundations but lacks corpus caveats about line count and long-text default wrapping
Motion performance, transition specificity, press scale, and AnimatePresence first-render suppression are already strongly covered
sk-design hub is routing-only and must not receive per-mode design logic
mode registry confirms five target homes: interface, foundations, motion, audit, md-generator
motion mode already contains strong matching rules for interruptibility, bounce-zero springs, press scale, AnimatePresence first render, and performance failures
foundations type already covers text-wrap balance/pretty and tabular numerals but not root font smoothing

reason: sk-design already keeps output and workflow behavior in mode packets; importing a global table would conflict with mode-owned contracts.

direction: Put make-interfaces-feel-better techniques into sk-design hub
reason: The hub is routing-only; per-mode logic belongs in mode packets or shared vocabulary.
